name: Colmenar Viejo, Pedrezuela, Soto del Real – circular route
name_original: Colmenar Viejo, Pedrezuela, Soto del Real – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Colmenar Viejo offer diverse terrain at the foot of the Sierra de Guadarrama, approximately 30 kilometers north of Madrid. The region features rolling hills, extensive oak and pine woodlands, and agricultural fields, providing varied landscapes for cyclists. A significant portion of the area is protected within the Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares, ensuring natural beauty along the routes. Cyclists can expect a mix of well-paved surfaces and some unpaved segments, with routes ranging from moderate to difficult. Best road cycling routes around Colmenar Viejo The most popular road cycling route is Alto del Cerro de San Pedro – Puerto de Canencia climb loop from Colmenar Viejo, an 80.8 miles (130.1 km) difficult trail that takes 6 hours 44 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Colmenar Viejo, Pedrezuela, Soto del Real – circular route, a moderate 36.3 miles (58.5 km) path connecting several towns with varied scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Alto del Cerro de San Pedro – Soto del Real Cycling Area loop from Colmenar Viejo, a 40.7 miles (65.5 km) trail leading through the cycling area, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around Colmenar Viejo is defined by rolling hills, oak and pine woodlands, and the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging climbs. The routes in Colmenar Viejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 4000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Colmenar Viejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Colmenar Viejo?
A: Road cycling routes around Colmenar Viejo feature diverse terrain, including rolling hills, extensive oak and pine woodlands, and agricultural fields. The area is situated at the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama, offering varied landscapes. You'll find a mix of well-paved surfaces, though some routes may include unpaved segments.

Q: What are the difficulty levels for road cycling routes in Colmenar Viejo?
A: The routes in Colmenar Viejo cater to various fitness levels, ranging from moderate to difficult. Out of approximately 27 available routes, you can find around 4 easy, 10 moderate, and 13 difficult options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14947640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Cerro de San Pedro – Puerto de Canencia climb loop from Colmenar Viejo&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several routes suitable for beginners. Approximately 4 of the routes are classified as easy, offering a gentler introduction to road cycling in the region. These routes typically have less elevation gain and shorter distances, allowing for a more relaxed experience.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while road cycling near Colmenar Viejo?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, with much of it protected within the Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares. You might encounter prominent peaks like Cerro de San Pedro and Cerro Longo, or the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/484448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrezuela Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The Manzanares River also traverses the municipality, offering scenic views along its protected margins.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, beyond the natural landscapes, you can explore several historical attractions. Notable sites include the Basilica of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción in Colmenar Viejo itself, and the well-preserved &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/434252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares el Real Castle&lt;/a&gt; nearby. Historical bridges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2555399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Bridge&lt;/a&gt; are also part of some routes.

Q: Can I find routes with scenic viewpoints in Colmenar Viejo?
A: Absolutely. The varied terrain and rolling hills often lead to panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/957559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeliganar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is one such spot that offers expansive views of the surrounding landscape. Many routes, especially those climbing to higher elevations, provide excellent opportunities for scenic stops.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Colmenar Viejo are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977087270/colmenar-viejo-pedrezuela-soto-del-real-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colmenar Viejo, Pedrezuela, Soto del Real – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which connects several towns with varied scenery.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Colmenar Viejo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging climbs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canencia Pass – Puerto de Canencia climb loop&lt;/a&gt; to scenic rides through oak and pine woodlands, and the overall natural beauty at the foot of the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Is there a dedicated cycle path from Madrid to Colmenar Viejo?
A: Yes, a dedicated cycle path extends north from Madrid alongside the M-607 motorway. This offers a car-free and quiet cycling experience, though it presents a gradual, long uphill gradient when heading out from Madrid towards Colmenar Viejo.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: While not directly on a road cycling route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; on the Guadalix River is a notable natural feature in the region. It's nestled in a rocky canyon and can be reached via a flat and easy hiking route, making it a potential side trip or a destination for a multi-sport day.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Colmenar Viejo?
A: Colmenar Viejo offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, especially at midday, while winter rides are possible but may require appropriate gear for cooler temperatures, particularly at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any moderate routes for cyclists looking for a good workout without extreme difficulty?
A: Yes, a significant portion of the routes, around 10 out of 27, are classified as moderate. These routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39563539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Repsol Picnic Area – Soto del Real Cycling Area loop from Colmenar Viejo&lt;/a&gt;, offer a good balance of distance and elevation gain, providing a satisfying challenge without the intensity of the most difficult climbs.
tips: Take a moment to appreciate the beautiful views from this place.
Guadalix de la Sierra is the town in Madrid where "Welcome Mr. Marshall" by Luis García Berlanga was filmed in 1952.

Soto is one of the epicenters of cycling in the Sierra de Guadarrama. From here you can access many roads and tracks that lead to the mountain itself. Its leisure and restaurant offerings have made it the origin and end of long days by bike and beers and after-meals reminiscing about the adventure.

waypoints.name: Plaque Commemorating Luis García Berlanga and 'Bienvenido Mr. Marshall' in Guadalix de la Sierra
